<!--
Copyright (c) 2026 Dhruv Azad. All rights reserved.
Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Dhruv Azad
-->


---
title: "Fixed Point Iteration"
page-layout: full
---

```{=html}
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/mathjs@13.2.0/lib/browser/math.js"></script>
<script src="https://cdn.plot.ly/plotly-2.35.2.min.js"></script>
<script src="../../js/utils/math-expression.js"></script>
<script src="../../js/utils/plot-model.js"></script>
<script src="../../js/utils/plotly-traces.js"></script>
<script src="../../js/utils/render-utils.js"></script>
```

Explore fixed point iteration through a cobweb diagram. Enter an iteration function \(g(x)\), choose an initial guess, and watch the sequence approach or move away from a fixed point.

<div class="ojs-controls-wide">

### Controls

<div class="ojs-control-grid">

```{ojs}
viewof gText = Inputs.text({
  label: "Iteration function g(x)",
  value: "cos(x)",
  placeholder: "Example: cos(x), sqrt(x + 2), exp(-x)"
})
```

```{ojs}
viewof x0 = Inputs.number({
  label: "Initial guess x₀",
  value: 1,
  step: 0.1
})
```

```{ojs}
viewof tolerance = Inputs.number({
  label: "Tolerance",
  value: 1e-6,
  step: 1e-6
})
```

```{ojs}
viewof maxIterations = Inputs.range([1, 100], {
  label: "Maximum iterations",
  value: 30,
  step: 1
})
```

</div>

```{ojs}
viewof stepControl = {
  const div = html`
    <div class="ojs-button-row">
      <button class="ojs-next-button">Next iteration</button>
      <button class="ojs-reset-button">Reset</button>
      <span style="color:#475569;font-weight:600;"></span>
    </div>
  `

  const nextButton = div.querySelector(".ojs-next-button")
  const resetButton = div.querySelector(".ojs-reset-button")
  const label = div.querySelector("span")

  let value = 0
  div.value = value
  label.textContent = `Current step: ${value}`

  nextButton.onclick = () => {
    value = value + 1
    div.value = value
    label.textContent = `Current step: ${value}`
    div.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("input"))
  }

  resetButton.onclick = () => {
    value = 0
    div.value = value
    label.textContent = `Current step: ${value}`
    div.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("input"))
  }

  return div
}
```

</div>

<div class="ojs-output-wide">

```{ojs}
mathjs = window.math
```

```{ojs}
expressionUtils = window.VisualMathExpressionUtils
```

```{ojs}
plotModelUtils = window.VisualMathPlotModelUtils
```

```{ojs}
plotlyTraceUtils = window.VisualMathPlotlyTraceUtils
```

```{ojs}
renderUtils = window.VisualMathRenderUtils
```

```{ojs}
compiledG = {
  return expressionUtils.tryCompileExpression(mathjs, gText)
}
```

```{ojs}
g = x => expressionUtils.evaluateCompiledExpression(compiledG, {x})
```

```{ojs}
computeFixedPointResult = ({start, tol, maxIter}) => {
  const rows = []

  if (!compiledG) {
    return {
      ok: false,
      statusType: "bad",
      message: "Could not parse g(x). Try an expression such as cos(x), exp(-x), or sqrt(x + 2).",
      rows
    }
  }

  if (!Number.isFinite(start)) {
    return {
      ok: false,
      statusType: "bad",
      message: "The initial guess x₀ must be a finite number.",
      rows
    }
  }

  let x = start

  for (let i = 0; i < maxIter; i++) {
    const gx = g(x)

    if (!Number.isFinite(gx)) {
      rows.push({
        i,
        x,
        gx,
        error: NaN
      })

      return {
        ok: false,
        statusType: "bad",
        message: `Iteration stopped because g(x_${i}) is not finite.`,
        rows
      }
    }

    const error = Math.abs(gx - x)

    rows.push({
      i,
      x,
      gx,
      error
    })

    if (error < tol) {
      return {
        ok: true,
        statusType: "good",
        message: `Converged after ${i + 1} iteration${i + 1 === 1 ? "" : "s"}. Approximate fixed point: x ≈ ${gx.toPrecision(10)}.`,
        rows
      }
    }

    x = gx
  }

  return {
    ok: true,
    statusType: "warn",
    message: `Reached the maximum number of iterations. Last approximation: x ≈ ${x.toPrecision(10)}.`,
    rows
  }
}
```

```{ojs}
fixedPointResult = computeFixedPointResult({
  start: Number(x0),
  tol: Number(tolerance),
  maxIter: Number(maxIterations)
})
```

```{ojs}
fixedPointPlotModel = {
  const visibleRows = plotModelUtils.sliceVisibleRows(fixedPointResult.rows, stepControl)
  const rangeValues = []
  plotModelUtils.pushFiniteValues(rangeValues, [Number(x0)])

  for (const row of visibleRows) {
    plotModelUtils.pushFiniteValues(rangeValues, [row.x, row.gx])
  }

  const range = plotModelUtils.createPaddedRange(rangeValues, {
    emptyRange: [-2, 2],
    singularPadding: 2,
    relativePadding: 0.45,
    minPadding: 1.0
  })

  return {
    visibleRows,
    range
  }
}
```

```{ojs}
visibleRows = fixedPointPlotModel.visibleRows
```

```{ojs}
fixedPointPlotRange = fixedPointPlotModel.range
```

```{ojs}
fixedPointBaseTraces = {
  const lo = fixedPointPlotRange.lo
  const hi = fixedPointPlotRange.hi
  const n = 500
  const xs = Array.from({length: n}, (_, i) => lo + (hi - lo) * i / (n - 1))
  const ys = xs.map(x => g(x)).map(y => Number.isFinite(y) ? y : null)

  return [
    plotlyTraceUtils.createLineTrace({
      x: xs,
      y: ys,
      name: "g(x)",
      color: "#2563eb",
      width: 3
    }),
    plotlyTraceUtils.createLineTrace({
      x: [lo, hi],
      y: [lo, hi],
      name: "y = x",
      color: "#111827",
      width: 2,
      dash: "dash"
    }),
    plotlyTraceUtils.createLineTrace({
      x: [lo, hi],
      y: [0, 0],
      name: "y = 0",
      color: "#94a3b8",
      width: 1,
      showlegend: false
    })
  ]
}
```

```{ojs}
fixedPointCobwebTraces = {
  if (visibleRows.length === 0) return []

  const cobwebX = []
  const cobwebY = []
  const start = Number(x0)

  if (Number.isFinite(start)) {
    cobwebX.push(start)
    cobwebY.push(start)
  }

  for (const row of visibleRows) {
    if (!Number.isFinite(row.x) || !Number.isFinite(row.gx)) continue

    cobwebX.push(row.x)
    cobwebY.push(row.gx)
    cobwebX.push(row.gx)
    cobwebY.push(row.gx)
  }

  return [
    plotlyTraceUtils.createLineTrace({
      x: cobwebX,
      y: cobwebY,
      name: "Cobweb path",
      color: "#dc2626",
      width: 2,
      mode: "lines+markers",
      marker: {
        color: "#dc2626",
        size: 7
      }
    })
  ]
}
```

```{ojs}
fixedPointIterateTraces = {
  if (visibleRows.length === 0) return []

  return [
    plotlyTraceUtils.createMarkerTrace({
      x: visibleRows.map(row => row.x).filter(Number.isFinite),
      y: visibleRows.map(row => row.x).filter(Number.isFinite),
      name: "Iterates",
      text: visibleRows
        .filter(row => Number.isFinite(row.x))
        .map(row => `x_${row.i}`),
      textposition: "top center",
      color: "#16a34a",
      size: 9
    })
  ]
}
```

```{ojs}
fixedPointPlot = {
  const div = html`<div class="plotly-box-large"></div>`

  const lo = fixedPointPlotRange.lo
  const hi = fixedPointPlotRange.hi
  const data = [
    ...fixedPointBaseTraces,
    ...fixedPointCobwebTraces,
    ...fixedPointIterateTraces
  ]

  const layout = {
    title: {
      text: "Fixed Point Iteration Cobweb Diagram"
    },
    margin: {
      l: 60,
      r: 30,
      t: 60,
      b: 55
    },
    xaxis: {
      title: "x",
      range: [lo, hi],
      zeroline: true
    },
    yaxis: {
      title: "y",
      range: [lo, hi],
      zeroline: true,
      zerolinecolor: "#94a3b8",
      gridcolor: "#e2e8f0",
      linecolor: "#cbd5e1"
    },
    legend: {
      orientation: "h",
      y: -0.18
    },
    hovermode: "closest"
  }

  Plotly.newPlot(div, data, layout, {
    responsive: true,
    displaylogo: false
  })

  return div
}
```

```{ojs}
fixedPointStatus = {
  return renderUtils.renderStatusBox({
    html,
    statusType: fixedPointResult.statusType,
    message: fixedPointResult.message
  })
}
```

```{ojs}
fixedPointTable = {
  const rows = visibleRows

  if (rows.length === 0) {
    return renderUtils.renderEmptyState(html, "No iterations to display yet.")
  }

  return html`
  <div class="ojs-table-wrap"> 
    <table class="ojs-table"> 
      <thead> 
        <tr> 
          <th>n</th> 
          <th>x_n</th> 
          <!-- Use the native OJS tex tag here -->
          <th>${tex`g(x_n) = x_{n+1}`}</th> 
          <th>${tex`|x_{n+1} - x_n|`}</th> 
        </tr> 
      </thead> 
      <tbody> 
        ${rows.map(row => html` 
          <tr> 
            <td>${row.i}</td> 
            <td>${Number.isFinite(row.x) ? row.x.toPrecision(10) : "NaN"}</td> 
            <td>${Number.isFinite(row.gx) ? row.gx.toPrecision(10) : "NaN"}</td> 
            <td>${Number.isFinite(row.error) ? row.error.toExponential(4) : "NaN"}</td> 
          </tr> 
        `)} 
      </tbody> 
    </table> 
  </div> 
  `


}
```

</div>

## Explanation

Fixed point iteration is a way to solve an equation after rewriting it in the form

$$
x=g(x).
$$

Starting with an initial guess $x_0$, the method repeatedly applies $g$:

$$
x_{n+1}=g(x_n).
$$

If the sequence settles down to a value $\alpha$, then that value satisfies

$$
\alpha=g(\alpha).
$$

So $\alpha$ is called a fixed point of $g$.

## What the picture is showing

The plot shows two curves:

- the function $y=g(x)$,
- the line $y=x$.

The red path shows the iteration. From the current point, the method moves vertically to the curve $y=g(x)$, then horizontally to the line $y=x$. Repeating this creates the cobweb pattern.

This visualization is useful because it shows whether the iteration appears to be moving toward a fixed point, moving away from it, or oscillating.

A common condition that helps guarantee local convergence is

$$
|g'(x)|<1
$$

near the fixed point.
